title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Devikulam, a former double-member constituency, has a unique history

The constituency with a Tamil legacy has seen several Tamil leaders, including MGR, coming to campaign for elections

title: Kerala polls: After the K.M. Mani era, Pala faces its most open contest yet
content: While the incumbent MLA Mani C. Kappen of the UDF is confident of a repeat win, Jose K. Mani is banking on the heavy organisational depth of KC(M) to carry forward K.M. Mani’s success story in Pala. The BJP is vying to expand its base through Shone George by winning over Catholic votes

content: Iran is one of the world's biggest oil producers. The country holds the world's second-largest proven natural gas reserves and the third-largest crude oil reserves, according to United States Energy Information Administration.

title: Jaish Chief Masood Azhar's Brother Dies In Pakistan. Cause Of Death Unknown
content: The banned terrorist group's official channel said Mohammad Tahir Anwar's funeral was scheduled to be held in Pakistan's Bahawalpur.

title: Light Rain And Thunderstorms Expected In Delhi Today
content: New Delhi, March 31 (IANS) Very light rain or drizzle is expected in Delhi on Tuesday, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), which forecasts a generally cloudy sky and intermittent weather activity throughout the day.

title: Air Canada CEO To Quit Over "English-Only" Message After New York Plane Crash
content: Canada's largest airline is subject to the Official Languages Act, requiring it to provide equal services in English and French.

title: "Lied, Strung Us Along Negotiations": US On Killing Iran Leaders Amid War
content: The White House's remarks came after Iran said it had received US peace proposals but called them "unrealistic, illogical and excessive".

title: 8 women killed, 6 injured in stampede at Bihar's Sheetalashtami temple
content: Tragedy struck Nalanda's Maghra area as a stampede at Mata Sheetalashtami Temple claimed eight lives and injured several devotees. The incident occurred during a large religious gathering, causing panic. Meanwhile, President Droupadi Murmu is set to grace Nalanda University's convocation in Rajgir, marking her first visit as President to the institution.

title: 'US respects Israel's right': Netanyahu govt approves death penalty for Palestinian attackers
content: Israel's parliament has approved a controversial law allowing the execution of Palestinians convicted of deadly terror attacks, alongside a crucial national budget. Prime Minister Netanyahu backed the bill, which critics argue creates unequal legal tracks. This dual move comes amid ongoing regional conflict and rising violence in the West Bank.

title: ‘Baat nahi hui’: Why Ravindra Jadeja hasn’t spoken to MS Dhoni yet
content: Ravindra Jadeja impressed on his Rajasthan Royals return, taking two crucial wickets against his former team Chennai Super Kings. He humorously revealed he hadn't spoken to MS Dhoni, who apparently keeps his phone off. Jadeja enjoyed the turning wicket, executing his plans effectively to help RR secure an emphatic victory.

title: Gold price prediction: What levels of yellow metal should investors watch out for?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold is trading as a risk asset and not a safe haven asset, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: China CCTV ban reshapes market: India shuts out Hikvision, Dahua
content: India has effectively barred Chinese surveillance giants Hikvision and Dahua from selling internet-connected CCTV cameras, shifting market dominance to domestic brands. This move, driven by new quality certification rules, has seen Indian companies capture over 80% of the market, though at increased costs for premium models.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ inches close to Rs 1,400 crore worldwide in 12 days
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has achieved massive box office success, becoming his highest-grossing film ever. The spy thriller sequel surpassed its predecessor's records, earning over Rs 800 crore net in India and Rs 1300 crore gross worldwide. Despite a weekday dip, the film's strong performance continues to impress.

title: 'Two-year ban rok di': How Mohsin Naqvi saved Naseem Shah's career in last-minute call
content: Pakistan cricketer Naseem Shah narrowly avoided a two-year ban for a controversial social media post, with PCB chief Mohsin Naqvi reportedly intervening to impose a PKR 20 million fine instead. The pacer had criticized Punjab Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz's presence at a PSL game.

title: Assam assembly elections: What is BJP offering in its poll manifesto
content: The BJP's Assam election manifesto, 'Sankalp Patra,' outlines 31 commitments focusing on indigenous rights, economic growth, and infrastructure. Key promises include investing Rs 5 lakh crore to make Assam an economic hub, strengthening law and order, and implementing a Uniform Civil Code. The party also pledges to protect land and heritage of indigenous communities and create jobs for youth.

title: Microsoft AI CEO reveals what will really decide AI's winners and losers by 2028
content: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman argues that the AI industry's future hinges on companies that can afford to run models at scale, not just build the smartest ones. He posits that inference compute scarcity will define winners and losers for the next few years, with high-margin products gaining a significant advantage through a data flywheel of continuous improvement and adoption.

title: Willing to end war without re-opening Hormuz, Trump tells aides as Iran refuses to budge
content: President Trump is reportedly willing to end the conflict without reopening the Strait of Hormuz, opting instead to weaken Iran's military capabilities and increase diplomatic pressure. This shift comes as an operation to unblock the strait was deemed likely to extend the conflict beyond his preferred timeline.

title: Candidates Chess: How Divya Deshmukh missed winning chance against R Vaishali
content: The FIDE Candidates 2026 saw a day of draws in Cyprus, with all games ending in a tie. Indian talents Divya Deshmukh and Vaishali Rameshbabu played a thrilling game, featuring a brilliant queen sacrifice by Vaishali. In the Open section, R Praggnanandhaa drew against Wei Yi, while Hikaru Nakamura salvaged a draw from a difficult position.

title: Starting April 1, USCIS alert says that the immigration authority will reject forms
content: USCIS will only accept the 02/27/26 edition of Form I-129 for H-1B and other visa applications starting April 1, 2026. The updated form requires employers to detail educational requirements and experience, aligning with the Department of Labor's wage level assignments for the new wage-weighted lottery system.

title: ‘DJ music muffled his cries’: UP woman, lover sedate husband, tie him to cot, slit throat
content: Police in Bijnor have arrested a woman, her lover, and his friend for the murder of her husband, Mohammad Mehraj. The victim was allegedly sedated and then murdered with a sharp weapon while sleeping. The wife reportedly staged being unconscious during the incident.

title: Trump shares video of massive explosions in Iran's Isfahan; bunker-busters used?
content: US President Donald Trump intensified his rhetoric on the military campaign against Iran, sharing a striking image of Isfahan ablaze following Operation Epic Fury. American forces reportedly used powerful bunker-buster bombs on a large ammunition depot in the city, which also houses facilities linked to Iran's nuclear program. This follows previous strikes targeting its nuclear infrastructure.

title: Florida airport to be renamed after Donald Trump
content: Palm Beach International Airport is officially set to become President Donald J. Trump International Airport following a Florida bill signed by Governor Ron DeSantis. Eric Trump announced the change, which takes effect July 1st, pending procedural updates. The Trump Organization filed a trademark for the name, assuring no financial benefit to the former president or his family from the rebranding.

title: Russian photographer’s ‘pink elephant’ shoot in Jaipur sparks animal safety concern
content: A Russian photographer's viral photoshoot featuring a bright pink-painted elephant in Jaipur has sparked online criticism and ethical concerns. The photographer stated organic, locally-sourced paint was used and the elephant was cleaned promptly after the shoot. The concept aimed to reflect Rajasthan's vibrant culture and the animal's significance.

title: Karnataka 1st PUC results 2026 declared; state board releases marks memo via official portal
content: The Karnataka School Examination and Assessment Board announced the Karnataka 1st PUC Result 2026 on March 31, enabling Class 11 students to access marks memos online via the official portal. Candidates can download their scorecards using required credentials and verify details. The board will open applications for re-evaluation and answer script scrutiny within a specified period. Students who fail in subjects can appear for supplementary examinations, with the schedule expected to be released soon.

title: TVK chief Vijay booked for violating election rules day after entering Tamil Nadu polls
content: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) chief Vijay faces a case for alleged election conduct violations. A complaint cited the use of 30 loudspeakers and a gathering of over 5,000 people causing public inconvenience and obstructing a pathway, reportedly blocking an ambulance. Police have registered a case against Vijay and party workers.

title: Bank holiday: Are banks open or closed today for Mahavir Jayanti? Check state-wise list
content: Many banks across India are closed today for Mahavir Jayanti, impacting customers in states like Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Uttar Pradesh. However, agency banks handling government transactions will remain open on March 31st to facilitate financial year-end dealings. Digital banking services are unaffected, but check with your bank for specific details.

title: Why Vaibhav Sooryavanshi said no to 'birthday cake' before smashing CSK
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi starred in Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 opener, smashing a rapid 52 off 17 balls against Chennai Super Kings.  He prioritized rest over birthday celebrations to deliver a match-winning powerplay performance, guiding his team to an emphatic eight-wicket victory.  The young sensation credited his coaches and opening partner for his confident display.

title: 'Didn't expect this': American tourist in India reflects on a journey in India that changed him
content: An American tourist shares his transformative journey in India. He found unexpected kindness amidst the chaos. This experience deeply affected him, making him feel a sense of belonging. He emphasizes that India is not just visited but felt. The memories and feelings stay with him long after his departure.

title: 'Agastya, are you ok?' Hardik Pandya's girlfriend Mahieka snaps at photographers
content: Hardik Pandya's girlfriend, Mahieka Sharma, protected cricketer's son Agastya from intrusive photographers after a Mumbai Indians match.  She shielded the child from camera flashes, visibly annoyed by the crowd's behavior. Meanwhile, Mumbai Indians secured a thrilling six-wicket victory against Kolkata Knight Riders, marking their first season-opening win since 2012.

title: 'India a better mediator than Pakistan': Israeli envoy on Middle East war
content: Israel's envoy dismisses Pakistan's mediation efforts in the Middle East conflict, favoring India as a 'better mediator.' She highlighted significant military gains against Iran's proxies and internal instability within Iran. The envoy also emphasized a dual-track US strategy of diplomacy and military pressure, ruling out compromise on Iran's nuclear program.

title: ‘Kalkaji temple among targets’: LeT man’s arrest exposes Kolkata base for Delhi attacks
content: Delhi Police arrested Lashkar-e-Taiba commander Shabbir Ahmed Lone, a 'most wanted' operative, after a month-long operation. Lone, who allegedly ran a cross-border terror network from Bangladesh on ISI's directions, was instrumental in radicalizing youth for terror attacks. His arrest effectively disrupts a revived terror pipeline.

title: White House ballroom project to sit atop underground military complex
content: The US military is constructing a large underground complex beneath a new, privately funded White House ballroom, which will house an upgraded Presidential Emergency Operations Center. President Trump confirmed the project, highlighting advanced security features for the ballroom. Reports indicate that the classified nature of the underground work allowed it to bypass approval by the National Capital Planning Commission

title: Celtics vs Hawks injury report: Who's playing, injured and questionable players
content: Boston Celtics, on a three-game win streak, face the surging Atlanta Hawks in a crucial playoff seeding clash. Both teams grapple with key player injuries, making team depth and rotation pivotal. Boston's Jayson Tatum, Jaylen Brown, and Derrick White are game-time decisions, while Atlanta monitors Dyson Daniels, Onyeka Okongwu, and Jonathan Kuminga.

title: TJ Watt’s $123M deal criticized as Aaron Rodgers eyes new contract
content: The Pittsburgh Steelers face a significant financial challenge in the 2026 offseason. With T.J. Watt's hefty contract and Aaron Rodgers seeking a substantial raise, the Steelers' cap space is severely strained. Watt's production has declined, raising questions about his value, while Rodgers' strong performance has inflated his market, forcing a tough decision on which star to prioritise. Now, they must choose. Stick with a high-priced defence led by Watt or adjust the books to bring Rodgers back. Either way, the margin for error is gone.

title: MLB trade rumors: San Diego Padres eye $38.5M Boston Red Sox Star to fix rotation
content: The San Diego Padres are eyeing Lucas Giolito for their 2026 baseball season rotation. With Yu Darvish's salary off the books, the team has funds to acquire a reliable pitcher. Giolito offers stability to a rotation needing depth. A potential two-year deal provides flexibility for both the Padres and Giolito.

title: PSL controversy: Afridi fined for 'forcefully escorting' 4 visitors to hotel room
content: Pakistan pacer Shaheen Afridi has been fined PKR 1 million by Lahore Qalandars for breaching hotel security protocols during the PSL. He and teammate Sikandar Raza reportedly hosted guests in Raza's room late at night, despite explicit refusal from authorities. The franchise acknowledged the incident, citing a misunderstanding.

title: Collared tiger killed to protect 'secret' opium farm in MP
content: Illegal opium cultivators killed a rewilded, radio-collared tiger in Madhya Pradesh to deter forest patrols from discovering their illicit farms. The big cat's movements, tracked by its collar, brought officials too close, prompting the deadly act. The tiger was poisoned using a bull, and its collar was destroyed to hide evidence.

title: Iran strikes fully loaded Kuwaiti oil tanker at Dubai port; spill risk flagged
content: A Kuwaiti crude oil tanker anchored off Dubai was targeted in a direct Iranian attack, igniting a fire and damaging its hull. This incident, involving drone strikes, follows a pattern of attacks on commercial shipping in the Gulf. Dubai authorities are managing the blaze, with all 24 crew members reported safe.

title: Stock market holiday today: Are BSE, NSE closed for trading on March 31, 2026?
content: This holiday is the fifth among the 16 market closures planned for 2026. Trading will remain suspended again on April 3 (Friday) for Good Friday, followed by another holiday on April 14 (Tuesday) to mark Dr. B.R. Ambedkar Jayanti.

title: Pentagon rejects report that Hegseth attempted defence investments before Iran attack
content: A report alleged a broker explored defense investments before US-Israeli strikes on Iran. Sean Parnell, assistant to US War Secretary Pete Hegseth, has vehemently denied these claims. He stated the allegations are false and fabricated. Parnell demanded an immediate retraction of the report. Secretary Hegseth and the Department of War maintain their commitment to ethics and regulations.

title: Vizag: Navy staffer kills lover, stores torso in fridge, burns limbs in hills
content: In a shocking turn of events, a married officer from the Indian Navy in Visakhapatnam faces arrest for the brutal murder of a woman. Authorities allege he dismembered her body, hiding some parts in his residence and incinerating others. The victim's father refutes the man's assertions of financial motives, claiming the crime was an attempt to safeguard his professional reputation.

title: Oil prices today: Crude jumps after war disrupts supply routes, global shortage fear looms
content: Oil prices have jumped significantly. Brent crude is over $110 and WTI is above $100. Supply worries are growing due to the conflict in the Middle East. Iran's actions and Houthi missile strikes are impacting key shipping routes. This situation threatens global energy flows. Saudi Arabia is rerouting its oil. The US has warned Iran about its energy infrastructure.

title: Burqa-clad Hindu woman stopped at Varanasi airport on way to marry Muslim man, court lets her go
content: a 22-year-old woman was detained along with a youth at the lal Bahadur Shastri International Airport in Varanasi . the woman from Gopalganj in Bihar had to board a Mumbai flight with the youth . airline officials grew suspicious when they saw her in burqa . after initial questioning, CISF handed her and the youth accompanying her to police .

title: Transfer rumors, news: De Zerbi open to Spurs managerial job
content: 'FC TV' crew react to the 5-2 defeat to Belgium . Tottenham are hoping to convince Roberto De Zerbi to become their permanent head coach, sources say . former swansea boss has been linked with the role as have Sean Dyc .

title: Census 2027 starts April 1, digital mode means data will be available same year

title: Sabalenka excels, Sinner back on track and more takeaways from Indian Wells and Miami Open
content: the "sunshine double" and tennis' version of March Madness are elusive feats . just four women and seven men had achieved back-to-back victory in singles play . despite everything mentioned above, the Sunshine Double was achieved not just once over the weekend .

title: Kryptonite for 10 NBA title contenders: Which weaknesses could sink playoff teams?
content: in the east, the Detroit Pistons have already clinched a playoff berth in hopes of securing the conference's no. 1 seed . despite playoff contenders catapulting themselves over conference competition, each team possess a kryptonite that could lead to their playoff undoing . our insiders evaluated the biggest weakness of the top 10 NBA Finals contenders .

title: Previewing the McDonald's All American boys' game: Stokes vs. Branch, more to watch
content: the McDonald's All American boys' game annually brings together the nation's top prospects in the senior high school class . all 24 players in this year's game are ranked inside the ESPN 100 -- including the top seven players in the class, headlined by no. 1 Tyran Stokes and no. 2 Jordan Smith .

title: MMA heavyweight madness: Which big fella would win a 16-man tournament?
content: the men's and women's basketball tournaments haven't stopped us from creating our own hypothetical tournaments for the past few years . the big fellas will take center stage in this year's bracket . former uFC and PFL heavyweight champ Alex Pereira will move up to MMA's premier weight class on may 14 .

title: Early Men's Final Four preview: Arizona-Michigan, UConn-Illinois predictions
content: the 2026 men's final four is set for saturday's national semifinals against Illinois . the final weekend of the tournament is the final of the NCAA tournament . for the season, Arizona ranked fifth in the country in paint points, averaging 42 .
